stories.title: Treasury yields fall as investors digest week of bond market volatility
stories.summary: The yield on the 10-year U.S. Treasury note — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — fell more than 2 basis points to 4.564%.

stories.title: The Tech Download: What you might have missed in Nvidia’s earnings — a $200 billion opportunity and edge computing
stories.summary: While Jensen Huang said the chip giant had "conceded" the China market, Nvidia reported another blockbuster quarter on Wednesday.

stories.title: Gilt relief rally sends yields to biggest weekly drop since 2024
stories.summary: Burnham pledge to stick to fiscal rules and pullback from bets on higher BoE interest rates drive rebound

stories.title: Russia Is ‘Guiding’ Drones Into NATO Airspace, Estonia Says
stories.summary: Estonian Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna accused Russia of “guiding” stray Ukrainian military drones toward NATO countries after a series of airspace incursions caused disruption in the Baltic states this week.

stories.title: Samsung Chip Workers Face Colleagues’ Resentment Over Bonus Deal
stories.summary: Samsung Electronics Co. staved off a potentially catastrophic strike this week, reaching a tentative deal with leaders of its largest union at the last minute to share more profits from the AI boom. But inside the $1.1 trillion technology behemoth, not everyone is happy.

stories.title: Can Cuba’s Regime Survive Rising US Pressure?
stories.summary: Ever since US Special Forces captured Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro on Jan. 3 and launched an attack on Iran, Washington has been warning that Cuba will be next. Under a US-imposed economic blockade, the island has received almost no fuel in 2026. On May 13, Cuba’s energy minister said the country has completely run out of the diesel and fuel oil it needs to keep its power plants running.

stories.title: ‘A big mistake’: The ECB is ‘hell-bent’ on rate hikes despite recession risks, senior economist warns
stories.summary: Berenberg's chief economist said ECB rate rises would be a "big mistake" amid growing signs of European stagflation.

stories.title: AI Cartoon ‘Critterz’ Misses Cannes Debut After OpenAI Shut Sora
stories.summary: Critterz, a feature-length cartoon intended to showcase how OpenAI’s video-generation capabilities could revolutionize filmmaking, has missed its planned Cannes Film Festival debut after the artificial intelligence company shut down its Sora tool, forcing its creators to look for a new AI partner.

stories.title: U.S., Iran signal peace progress — but remain at odds over enriched uranium, Strait of Hormuz tolls
stories.summary: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Thursday said there were "good signs" that an agreement to end the Middle East conflict is in sight.

stories.title: EU-Anthropic Talks Over Mythos Concerns Are Stalled, Spain Says
stories.summary: The European Union has made little progress in talks with Anthropic PBC to get its banks and companies tested for digital vulnerabilities that the new Mythos AI model uncovers, Spain’s economy minister said.

stories.title: AI Capex Drives Memory Demand and Asia Tech Outlook
stories.summary: Rising investment in artificial intelligence is reshaping demand for semiconductors, with memory, CPUs and GPUs all seeing sustained growth. This segment examines how AI-driven capex is supporting companies across Asia’s tech supply chain. Jonathan Curtis, Portfolio Manager at Franklin Templeton, speaks with Haslinda Amin from the JPMorgan Global China Summit in Shanghai to share his outlook on AI

stories.title: Lenovo Surges to 26-Year High After Strong AI Growth | The China Show 5/22/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and David Ingles give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Costs Rise and Political Fallout Deepens Over Iran Stalemate
stories.summary: A month after an indefinite ceasefire, the endgame remains elusive

stories.title: US Touts ‘Slight Progress’ as Deal With Iran Remains in Limbo
stories.summary: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said there was “slight progress” in mediated negotiations with Iran, as the prospect of a deal to turn a fragile ceasefire into a lasting peace agreement remains uncertain.

stories.title: Italy Set to Prolong Fuel Aid Even as Iran War Weighs on Economy
stories.summary: Italy is set to extend its energy relief measures, with Industry Minister Adolfo Urso saying the support would remain in place as long as the Iran war pushes up fuel and energy prices for households and businesses.

stories.title: India RBI to Pay Record Dividend, Misses Market Estimates
stories.summary: India’s central bank will transfer 2.87 trillion rupees ($30 billion) to the government, a record but one that fell short of expectations even as the Iran war pushes up energy prices and strains public finances.

stories.title: Oil prices rise as traders grow skeptical of a breakthrough in U.S.-Iran talks
stories.summary: The U.S. and Iran have signaled progress in talks to end the war, but the warring sides remain at loggerheads over key issues.

stories.title: Puig plunges after Estée Lauder tie-up talks terminated; European stocks rise
stories.summary: European stocks pushed higher on Friday as investors monitor the U.S.-Iran war and a slew of economic data.

stories.title: EU Reviewing Italy’s Call to Ease Fiscal Rules as Costs Rise
stories.summary: The European Union is considering Italy’s plea to get more leeway on the bloc’s fiscal rules as the Iran war raises costs for households and businesses.

stories.title: Why Prabowo’s Commodity Export Controls Are Spooking Indonesian Markets
stories.summary: Indonesia’s President Prabowo Subianto has been undertaking an ambitious agenda to try to remake Southeast Asia’s biggest economy. The centerpiece is an effort to lift annual growth to 8%, up from an average of roughly 5% over the past decade.

stories.title: US-Iran Talks Progress Undermined By Hormuz, Uranium Divide
stories.summary: An attack on a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates has raised fears about the scope of Iran’s retaliation to a potential US resumption of strikes, with experts highlighting the greater role Tehran-backed militias in Iraq are playing in the war. Meanwhile, the US and Iran are seeking to turn a fragile ceasefire into a more permanent end to fighting, but have yet to reach an agreement. B

stories.title: Germany to Boost Raw-Materials Fund to Reduce Reliance on China
stories.summary: Germany plans to increase the firepower of its national raw-materials fund by as much as half to €1.5 billion ($1.7 billion) as it seeks to reduce dependence on critical supplies from countries such as China and shore up supply chains. Officials are also considering expanding the vehicle into a sovereign wealth fund from 2028 onward.

stories.title: ECB Chief Says Inflation Expectations Remain On Target
stories.summary: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde stressed that long-term inflation expectations are still broadly in line with the 2% target, despite deepening fallout from the Iran war.

stories.title: Euro-Zone Wage Growth Slowed Before War Drove Inflation Up
stories.summary: A key measure of euro-area pay growth slowed, providing some relief to European Central Bank officials worrying about emerging inflation risks from the Iran war.

stories.title: Crypto bros, scalpers and Logan Paul: Inside the world of Pokémon where cards are sold for millions

stories.title: UK borrowing rises and retail sales slide in April

stories.title: IMF Financial Counsellor on Global Economy, Cybersecurity
stories.summary: Tobias Adrian, the International Monetary Fund's Financial Counsellor and Monetary and Capital Markets Director, discusses the response by global economies to the supply shocks created by the Iran war as well as efforts to achieve a coordinated response to cyberattacks, as AI accelerates the threat. Adrian joined Lizzy Burden on Bloomberg Daybreak Europe. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.summary: Core inflation — which strips out prices of fresh food — was lower than the 1.7% expected by economists polled by Reuters and below the 1.8% reading in March.

stories.title: Kawasaki Heavy Shares Rally on AI Tie-Up Plan With Nvidia
stories.summary: Kawasaki Heavy Industries Ltd. shares rallied as much as 12%, the most since February, on the company’s plan to collaborate with US tech company Nvidia Corp. and others on physical AI robot technology.

stories.title: Trump said he'd 'remember' companies that didn't apply for tariff refunds. Many of them are anyway

stories.summary: Borrowing was higher than expected last month and retail sales fell as fuel prices surged.

stories.summary: Standard Chartered global CEO Bill Winters on Friday said he was “sorry” for comments describing “lower-value” workers, made while announcing job-cut plans earlier this week. Winters apologised in a LinkedIn post as he tried to cool debate among upset employees after he announced on Tuesday a 15 per cent cut of back-office staff at the lender by 2030. Winters said that AI adoption would replace so

stories.title: Warsh to Be Sworn in as Fed Chief During White House Ceremony
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh, who’s promised the biggest shakeup in decades at the US central bank, is set to be sworn into office Friday in a White House ceremony as the 17th chair of the Federal Reserve.

stories.title: Sprake: Markets Holding its Breath on Iran Shock
stories.summary: Asian stocks advanced on optimism the US-Iran talks could lead to a peace deal. Casey Sprake, Market Strategist at AG Capital spoke to Bloomberg’s Jennifer Zabasajja on Horizons Middle East & Africa on the market reaction to a potential deal. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Burnham Vows to Change UK Economy in Official Campaign Launch

stories.summary: Ukraine targeted the Yaroslavl oil refinery in central Russia overnight to inflict further damage on its foe’s energy infrastructure, according to President Volodymyr Zelenskyy.

stories.title: BlackRock's Li Sees Earnings Momentum Driven by AI
stories.summary: BlackRock Chief Investment Strategist Wei Li says artificial intelligence is driving a lot of the major earnings upgrades in equity markets. "We can look at AI lifting the whole market, but identifying where the bottlenecks are is a good active play," Li tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: Salesforce Touts AI Promise Over Reality in SaaSpocalypse Fight
stories.summary: Patients at the University of Chicago Medicine featured in a promotional video seamlessly refill prescriptions, book appointments and even get parking tips with the help of Agentforce, Salesforce Inc.’s flagship AI tool.

stories.title: China Launches Major Crackdown on Illegal Securities Trading
stories.summary: China has launched an unprecedented campaign against illegal cross-border trading to stem capital outflows, threatening severe penalties against popular brokers and ordering non-compliant accounts to be liquidated within two years.

stories.title: Randstad CEO on AI & Future of Work
stories.summary: Sander van ’t Noordende, CEO of recruitment giant Randstad, says their base case is that 7-8% of jobs will be replaced by artificial intelligence over the next 5 to 10 years. However, Van ‘t Noordende says he does not see the "doomsday scenario" that some predict. In an interview on "The Pulse with Francine Lacqua" on May 21, van ‘t Noordende said AI will be a "wave" over the next decade, but also

stories.title: Ifo Sees 'Lower Growth,' But No Recession in Germany
stories.summary: Ifo President Clemens Fuest discusses Germany's business outlook as an expectations index by the institute rose to 83.8 in May from a revised 83.5 the previous month. "Businesses are trying to adapt to this new situation," he says. Speaking on Bloomberg Television, Fuest adds: "We see lower growth in Germany, but we don't see a recession." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.title: China to Penalize Tiger, Futu in Cross-Border Flow Crackdown
stories.summary: China’s securities regulator plans to penalize Futu Holdings Ltd., UP Fintech Holding Ltd.’s Tiger Brokers and Longbridge Securities Ltd. for operating on the mainland without a license, marking a sharp escalation in a yearslong campaign against illicit cross-border brokerages.

stories.summary: Shares of Lenovo surged over 15% on Friday, after the electronics giant posted strong revenue growth bolstered by its artificial intelligence business.

The People’s Bank of China set the yuan’s midpoint rate, also known as the daily fixing rate, at 6.8349 to the US dollar on Thursday – the strongest showing since February 2023.
